notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET ‚Äî NOT YET RECORDED (interrupt not elicitable on the local LM Studio provider as of 2026-05-27; see scenarios.json by_adapter.opencode notes and assessment.json). User cancels a long in-flight opencode turn with the interrupt key (a bare Escape into the live TUI), then issues a recovery prompt that proves the parser reset. User-observable signal (scenario-meanings): ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready with state NOT sticking in working post-interrupt, plus a clean recovery arc on the SAME ses_‚Ä¶ session. OPENCODE-SPECIFIC daemon shape (see assessment.json): unlike pi, opencode's parser maps a step-finish part with reason=interrupted directly to turn_done (parser.go:158 `case "interrupted"`; watcher.go:435 isTerminalPart accepts `interrupted`), so the interrupt itself is observable as a clean working‚Üíready settle on the cancelled turn ‚Äî daemon_capability:full. This spec therefore asserts the interrupt-driven working‚Üíready (interrupt_done) on the same session, then a recovery ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc proving the parser reset. SESSION IDENTITY (opencode-specific): opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (agent.go) ‚Äî session state lives in the SQLite store and the daemon observes a UUID-keyed `ses_‚Ä¶` session. The live-TUI driver (run_live) holds ONE opencode TUI open in a tmux pane for the whole run, so the long-essay turn, the Escape interrupt, and the recovery turn all land on the SAME ses_‚Ä¶ store row (no /new, no restart). session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession (if the process scanner emits one) cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching the FIRST working in the recording, necessarily the rollout session ‚Äî the presession never enters working) establishes the session identity every later phase pins to via same_session_as. WHY NOT YET RECORDED (live evidence 2026-05-27): the `interrupt` step is now ported into drive-opencode-interactive.sh's run_live (oc_interrupt sends a bare Escape via `tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" Escape`), but two live attempts on the local LM Studio model produced ZERO step-finish reason=interrupted parts ‚Äî the essay turn either stalled in a pre-stream tool-call (ESC = no-op) or completed naturally on reason=stop before the interrupt window. The parser maps reason=interrupted ‚Üí turn_done in code (parser.go:158, test-covered), but the live TUI + local openai-compatible provider never PERSISTS an `interrupted` part for an ESC, so there is nothing for the daemon to observe. This spec is retained as the recordable target once a provider/model whose TUI-cancel actually writes a step-finish reason=interrupted is available (and oc_interrupt is hardened to assert an actual `interrupted` part rather than any terminal step-finish). HOW runs differ (once recorded): exact assistant wording (the cancelled essay text), per-turn token counts, how much streamed before the interrupt landed, the ses_‚Ä¶ UUID/PID, and sub-second timing all vary (model-output variance). Stable structurally: a ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc on ONE ses_‚Ä¶ session that does NOT stick in working post-interrupt, a clean recovery ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready on the same session, no waiting ever, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch (the ses_‚Ä¶ store session); never enters waiting (no tool use)
First prompt (long essay) drives the ses_‚Ä¶ session ready ‚Üí working (binds the rollout session; a transient presession never enters working)
PID bound to the rollout ses_‚Ä¶ session

User presses the interrupt key mid-turn; opencode writes a step-finish reason=interrupted, the parser maps it to turn_done, and the cancelled turn settles the SAME ses_‚Ä¶ session working ‚Üí ready ‚Äî state does NOT stick in working post-interrupt, and never entered waiting
The recovery prompt drives the SAME ses_‚Ä¶ session ready ‚Üí working, proving the parser reset after the interrupt
The recovery turn completes back to ready on the same ses_‚Ä¶ session; clean ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready arc, never entered waiting
Agent torn down once after the recovery turn ‚Üí a single process_exited on the rollout session; the live state immediately before teardown is ready
